semanticCore: Doubt, dispute, contention, milking, or debate
quranicRelevance: Doubting/arguing about truth (Miryah - 'fala taku fee miryatin'), disputing (Mumarat - 'fala tumari feehim'), and doubting/challenging ('afatumaroonahu').

keyDerivations.meaning: To dispute, debate, argue, or wrangle

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَفَتُمَارُونَهُ عَلَى مَا يَرَى (53:12): Do you then dispute/challenge him on what he saw? (challenging vision).
فَلَا تُمَارِ فِيهِمْ إِلَّا مِرَاءً: So do not argue/dispute concerning them except a brief argument.
discardedNoise: Milking a she-camel (مري) - original physical meaning.
Obscure details of horse galloping speeds.
summaryEn: The root signifies doubt and contention, warning against spiritual skepticism (Miryah) and futile disputation of historical accounts.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الشك والمخالفة، محذراً من الريب والشك العقدي (مرية) والمجادلة العمياء في الحقائق والوقائع التاريخية (يمارون).
rawText: التَّمَارِى 6 The disputing, or contending, together. (TA.) You say, هُمَا يَتَمَارَيَانِ بِالشِّعْرِ [They two dispute, or contend, together, with verses or poetry]. (TA in art. بده.) الرِّيحُ تَمْرِى السَّحَابَ 1 and 🡻تَمْتَرِيهِ The wind draws forth the clouds. (M, TA.) See an ex. in a verse cited voce عَزْلَآءُ. اِسْتَمْرَتِ الإِبِلُ المحَمْضَ 10 for اِسْتَمْرَأَتْهُ: see 1 in art. عدن. اِمْترَى 8 He doubted, به of it. (Kur, xliii. 61.) === See 1. مَارَاهُ 3 , inf. n. مُمَارَاةٌ and مِرَآءٌ, i. q. جَادَلَهُ; (S, K; *) He disputed with him, or did so obstinately, &c.: (TA:) it is only in opposing [what has been said; not in commencing a disputation]. (Msb.) مَرْوٌ A certain plant: see خَافُورٌ, and حَبَقٌ, and فَاخُورٌ.
